steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two independent clauses. The first clause ends with "frequency" and the second begins with the example about Austin-Bergstrom Airport. The connector must correctly punctuate the boundary between the two independent clauses and position "For example" as a transitional phrase introducing the second sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'frequency,for example.': Placing "for example" inside the first clause before a period makes it part of the first sentence, leaving the second clause without an introduction. This also creates a run-on because a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice B — 'frequency. For example,': A period ends the first independent clause cleanly. "For example," then opens the second sentence as a transitional phrase followed by a comma, which is correct Standard English. ✓
  • Choice C — 'frequency,for example;': A semicolon should connect two independent clauses, but here it follows the transitional phrase "for example," not an independent clause. This structure is grammatically incorrect. ✗
  • Choice D — 'frequency,for example,': Using commas on both sides of "for example" between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. A comma alone cannot separate two complete sentences. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
